topic: Skill / reusable-capability pattern in autonomous agent frameworks

sources.title: Voyager — An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
keyInsights: Coined 'skill library' concept for LLM-agent era; canonical runtime-learned skill architecture
Each skill indexed by embedding of its description; top-5 retrieval for new tasks
Automatic curriculum + iterative prompting + self-verification = skill library growth loop
3.3x more items discovered, 2.3x longer distance traveled, 15.3x faster tech-tree milestones vs prior SOTA

sources.title: Voyager source code — MineDojo/Voyager
keyInsights: Skills implemented as JavaScript functions executing through Mineflayer
skill_library/ directory; skills loaded via skill_library_dir parameter
Three agents: curriculum agent (maximize exploration), skill library (code storage/retrieval), iterative prompting mechanism
66.5% JS + 33.5% Python split; Node.js integration for Minecraft

sources.title: Voyager paper (Wang et al. NeurIPS 2023)
keyInsights: Formal academic description of skill library mechanics
Skills are 'temporally extended, interpretable, and compositional' code programs
Iterative prompting feedback loops: environment feedback, execution errors, self-verification

sources.title: JARVIS-1 — Open-World Multi-Task Agents with Memory-Augmented Multimodal LMs
keyInsights: Extends Voyager with multimodal memory (pre-trained knowledge + game survival experience)
Handles 200+ Minecraft tasks
Memory-augmented planning; closer to replay-buffer than explicit skill library

sources.title: ExpeL — LLM Agents Are Experiential Learners (2023)
keyInsights: Learns natural-language insights from experience without gradient updates
Mid-point between Voyager (code) and pure CLAUDE.md (prose)
Recall extracted insights at inference time

sources.title: SWE-agent paper (Yang et al. 2024)
keyInsights: Introduced Agent-Computer Interface (ACI) concept
ACI design significantly enhances agent's ability to edit code + navigate repos + run tests
Per-task tool surface parameterization via YAML config

sources.title: SWE-agent tool bundles reference
keyInsights: Tool bundles: structured folders with executables + config.yaml + install.sh
Bundles not called 'skills' but functionally equivalent to the Voyager-sense skill primitive

sources.title: SWE-agent /tools directory
keyInsights: 14 tool bundles: windowed, edit_anthropic, search, filemap, forfeit, image_tools, registry, submit, web_browser, ...
Each bundle has bin/ shell scripts + config.yaml + install.sh

sources.title: SWE-agent windowed tool bundle config
keyInsights: Declarative tool definition with signature, docstring, arguments
5 commands: goto, open, create, scroll_up, scroll_down — state queried via _state
JSON Schema-compatible declarative structure; harness builds tool registry at startup

sources.title: SWE-agent edit_anthropic bundle
keyInsights: Single-tool bundle: str_replace_editor clone of Anthropic's text_editor
Commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, undo_edit
Stateful via _state_anthropic

sources.title: OpenHands skills overview
keyInsights: Adopts 'extended AgentSkills standard' with optional keyword triggers
Three activation modes: always-on, keyword-triggered, agent-invoked
.agents/skills/ preferred; .openhands/skills/ and .openhands/microagents/ deprecated

sources.title: OpenHands microagents legacy docs
keyInsights: Microagents are deprecated terminology for skills
Migration path to .agents/skills/ documented
Three loading mechanisms: always-on, user-triggered, agent-invoked

sources.title: OpenHands keyword-triggered skills
keyInsights: Lexical keyword matching for skill activation
triggers: YAML frontmatter field — OpenHands-specific extension to Anthropic spec
Complementary to description-based activation

sources.title: OpenHands repo microagent (AGENTS.md)
keyInsights: AGENTS.md as repository-specific always-on context
Spectrum-adjacent to skills but not formally a skill

sources.title: OpenHands extensions registry
keyInsights: 45 extensions total: 36 skills + 9 plugins across 2 marketplaces
Plugins have hooks/ + scripts/ + SKILL.md; skills are pure markdown
Example skills: code-review, kubernetes, prd, ssh, github, jupyter
Also works as Claude Code plugin marketplace (cross-compatibility)

sources.title: OpenHands runtime plugins directory
keyInsights: Three plugins: agent_skills, jupyter, vscode
agent_skills = Python helper library called via IPythonRunCellAction (NOT function calling)
Critical disambiguation: 'agent_skills' plugin ≠ SKILL.md standard skills; same word, different thing

sources.title: OpenHands code-review SKILL.md example
keyInsights: Concrete example of OpenHands SKILL.md structure
Minimal frontmatter (name, description, triggers), extensive body with PERSONA + framework sections
Demonstrates 'skill as prompt-engineered persona' pattern common in autonomous contexts

sources.title: CodeAct paper — Wang et al. ICLR 2025
keyInsights: Python code as action space beats JSON function calling by ~20% on agent benchmarks
Foundation for OpenHands' IPython-as-skill-activation pattern
Argument for reconsidering function-calling as the universal tool interface

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK — Tools
keyInsights: Three skill-adjacent primitives: Agent.as_tool() (agent-as-skill), ToolSearchTool (schema lazy-load), Codex container.skill_id (spec mount)
defer_loading=True hides tool schemas until tool search retrieves them
No formally-named 'Skill' class in generic Agents SDK — skill concept is Codex-specific

sources.title: OpenAI Codex skills docs
keyInsights: Codex adopts agentskills.io standard directly
SKILL.md + scripts/ + references/ + assets/ + agents/openai.yaml (OpenAI-specific)
Progressive disclosure: metadata → instructions → resources
Two activation modes: explicit /skills command and implicit description-match

sources.title: OpenAI skills repository
keyInsights: Three categories: .system (auto-installed), .curated (vetted, installable), .experimental (early-stage)
Example: gh-address-comments for PR workflows
Primarily Python (76.7%); scripted automation focus

sources.title: Codex core-skills Rust crate
keyInsights: Reference library-layer skill primitive implementation
Types: AvailableSkills, SkillMetadata, SkillPolicy, SkillLoadOutcome, SkillMetadataBudget, SkillDependencyInfo
Modules: loader, manager, model, render, injection, config_rules, remote
detect_implicit_skill_invocation_for_command() for pattern-matched activation
Used by both Codex CLI (interactive) and Codex Cloud (autonomous) — skill primitive in the core crate

sources.title: OpenAI Codex Cloud docs
keyInsights: Codex Cloud: long-running background autonomous agents
Parallel task execution in managed cloud environments
Skills available via both CLI and Cloud surfaces

sources.title: Nous Research Hermes Function Calling
keyInsights: <tool_call> / <tool_response> ChatML tags for function calling
Hermes-3 GOAP (Goal-Oriented Action Planning) with <scratch_pad>
No skill primitive — pure function calling + recursive inference loop
No 'Claw' toolkit found in docs (confirmed with prior guide)

sources.title: Microsoft AutoGen tools
keyInsights: No skill concept; capabilities are BaseTool subclasses + FunctionTool + Workbench (MCP)
AssistantAgent register_for_execution is from older versions
Reusability via tool abstraction, not instruction abstraction

sources.title: Microsoft AutoGen design patterns
keyInsights: Component-based architecture: Model Clients, Tools, Code Executors, Workbench
Multi-agent design patterns via message protocols
No explicit skill domain concept

sources.title: Magentic-One — Microsoft Research
keyInsights: 5-agent architecture: Orchestrator + WebSurfer + FileSurfer + Coder + ComputerTerminal
Agent-as-skill-domain: 'encapsulating distinct skills in separate agents' — explicit OOP framing
Orchestrator two-loop design (task ledger outer, progress inner)
No formal skill registry; specialist agents are the skill primitive

sources.title: LangChain concepts / tools overview
keyInsights: No skill concept; Tool is the primary reusable capability abstraction
LangGraph subgraphs as subagent composition, not skills
Deep Agents mentioned as higher-level but not skill-based

sources.title: LangChain deepagents
keyInsights: Batteries-included agent harness on LangGraph
Tools: write_todos, read_file, write_file, edit_file, ls, glob, grep, execute, task (subagent)
No skill primitive; customization via tool addition and prompt customization
Auto-summarization for long contexts

sources.title: Pydantic-AI Toolsets
keyInsights: Toolset = bundle of tools + custom instructions + metadata + runtime composition
Composition patterns: CombinedToolset, FilteredToolset, PrefixedToolset, RenamedToolset, WrapperToolset
Skill-adjacent but doesn't support SKILL.md spec; no progressive disclosure
Strong candidate for adapter-based Agent Skills integration

sources.title: CrewAI skills documentation
keyInsights: Clean 2026 adoption of Agent Skills standard
Explicit 'Skills are NOT tools' distinction — most common confusion
Three-axis model: skills (how to think) + tools (what to do) + backstory (who you are)
Agent-level and crew-level scope; agent-level takes precedence
Pattern B: injection into task prompt (not a separate tool call)

sources.title: CrewAI agents
keyInsights: Pre-skill model: agents have role + goal + backstory + tools + LLM
Backstory is context/persona, not formally skills
Static capability configuration at initialization (no runtime accrual)

sources.title: Letta Code skills
keyInsights: Runtime skill creation via skill-creator skill — agent can write new SKILL.md mid-session
Multi-scope: global (~/.letta/skills/), agent-scoped (~/.letta/agents/{id}/skills/), project-scoped (.agents/skills/)
On-demand loading via tool call; skills live outside active memory for efficiency
Only production system that productionizes Voyager-style runtime skill accumulation (reduced to authored-skill-creation-as-a-skill)

sources.title: Letta framework (formerly MemGPT)
keyInsights: Stateful-agent platform with memory blocks + skills + subagents
Emphasizes advanced memory and continual learning
Bundles pre-built skills for memory + continual learning
Runtime capability extension in production

sources.title: Amp Agent Skills section
keyInsights: Pattern B: catalog-only, no dedicated Skill tool
Skills integrate transparently; bodies load on-demand
Storage precedence: .agents/skills/, ~/.config/agents/skills/, built-in
Skills can bundle mcp.json for per-skill MCP integration

sources.title: Firebender multi-agent skills
keyInsights: Runtime skill selection — agent chooses which to load based on task
Markdown files + YAML frontmatter
Android-coding domain specialization
Conditional autonomy via disable-model-invocation / user-invocable flags

sources.title: Factory CLI skills
keyInsights: Skills discoverable and composable — Droid chains multiple skills in a plan
Conditional autonomy: gate PRs but never merge without human review
Invocation via /skill-name or Droid choice
Reinforces skill-chaining as autonomous workflow primitive

sources.title: Databricks Genie skills
keyInsights: Data-platform vertical adopter; description-based auto-activation
Best-practice: narrow scope, step-by-step guidance, separated concerns (markdown vs scripts)
Workspace skills (shared) + user skills (personal)
Autonomous 'Agent mode' with automatic relevant-skill loading

sources.title: GitHub Copilot agent skills
keyInsights: Copilot adoption of Agent Skills open standard
Scopes: project (.github/skills/, .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/), personal (~/.copilot/skills/)
Organization/enterprise skills 'coming soon'
No autonomous-specific documentation yet

sources.title: Claude Platform Agent Skills overview
keyInsights: API-level skills: container: { skill_id } parameter mounts skills in code execution VM
Beta headers required: code-execution-2025-08-25, skills-2025-10-02, files-api-2025-04-14
Three skill types / three loading levels explicit
Custom Skills via /v1/skills endpoints; pre-built pptx/xlsx/docx/pdf skills
Cross-surface limitation: Skills don't sync between API / Claude.ai / Claude Code

sources.title: Claude Agent Skills best practices
keyInsights: Evaluation-driven development: build evals BEFORE writing skills
Concise-is-key; default assumption 'Claude is already smart'
Three degrees of freedom: high (text instructions), medium (pseudocode), low (specific scripts)
Test with all models you'll use; Haiku vs Sonnet vs Opus need different specificity
Claude-A-creates-skill / Claude-B-uses-skill iteration pattern
MCP tool references must be fully qualified (ServerName:tool_name) to avoid collisions

sources.title: agentskills.io — What are skills?
keyInsights: Three-stage progressive disclosure: Discovery → Activation → Execution
Skill benefits: self-documenting, extensible, portable (just files)
Official framing: 'lightweight, open format for extending AI agent capabilities'

sources.title: agentskills.io — Specification
keyInsights: Required frontmatter: name (max 64 chars), description (max 1024 chars)
Optional frontmatter: license, compatibility (max 500), metadata, allowed-tools (experimental)
Directory structure: SKILL.md + scripts/ + references/ + assets/
Three-tier progressive disclosure: metadata (~100 tokens) / instructions (<5000) / resources (unbounded)
Keep SKILL.md body under 500 lines; flatten references one level deep from SKILL.md

sources.title: agentskills.io — Adding skills support
keyInsights: Explicit autonomous-vs-HITL section on cloud-hosted agents (no user filesystem)
Trust considerations: project-level skills from untrusted repos — gate loading
Dedupe activations — avoid re-injection if skill already in context
Protect skill content from compaction — flag as non-prunable
Subagent delegation for complex skill workflows (optional advanced pattern)
Lenient validation recommended — warn on spec issues but load anyway

sources.title: Simon Willison — Claude Skills are awesome, maybe a bigger deal than MCP
keyInsights: 'General computer automation' framing — implicitly autonomous-leaning
MCP cost: 'tens of thousands of tokens of context'; skills: 'few dozen extra tokens per skill'
Composable skills scenario: data journalism + census + visualization + publishing
Predicts 'Cambrian explosion in Skills' — implies scalability for 100+ skills

sources.title: Anthropic blog — Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills
keyInsights: Oct 16 2025 engineering post — the canonical announcement
'General-purpose agents that interact with full-fledged computing environments' — autonomous framing
Progressive disclosure core principle; analogy to well-organized manual (TOC → chapters → appendix)
Claude Code as primary example of skill-enabled autonomous agent
